exercises.title: Classify as Event vs Command vs State
exercises.instructions:
Classify each:
A: PaymentInitiated(amount=5000, merchantId='MRC-001')
B: ProcessPayment(paymentId='PAY-001')
C: payment.status = 'PROCESSING'
D: OrderFilled(symbol='RELIANCE', qty=100, price=2800)
E: UpdateOrderStatus(orderId='ORD-1', status='FILLED')
exercises.expected_solution:
A: EVENT — PaymentInitiated
   Past tense: describes something that already happened
   Immutable: stored in event log forever
   Used to: rebuild PaymentAggregate state

B: COMMAND — ProcessPayment
   Present tense imperative: an instruction to do something
   May fail (validation, business rules)
   Results in an event if successful

C: STATE — payment.status = 'PROCESSING'
   Current in-memory value of the aggregate
   DERIVED from applying events
   Not stored in event sourcing (only events stored)

D: EVENT — OrderFilled
   Past tense: the order was filled (happened)
   Immutable: Zerodha trade audit trail
   Can reconstruct position from this event

E: COMMAND — UpdateOrderStatus
   Imperative: telling the system to update something
   In event sourcing: commands → validate → events (not direct updates!)
   CRUD pattern — in ES, you'd emit OrderStatusChanged event instead

test_cases.expected:
A: EVENT — PaymentInitiated
   Past tense: describes something that already happened
   Immutable: stored in event log forever
   Used to: rebuild PaymentAggregate state

B: COMMAND — ProcessPayment
   Present tense imperative: an instruction to do something
   May fail (validation, business rules)
   Results in an event if successful

C: STATE — payment.status = 'PROCESSING'
   Current in-memory value of the aggregate
   DERIVED from applying events
   Not stored in event sourcing (only events stored)

D: EVENT — OrderFilled
   Past tense: the order was filled (happened)
   Immutable: Zerodha trade audit trail
   Can reconstruct position from this event

E: COMMAND — UpdateOrderStatus
   Imperative: telling the system to update something
   In event sourcing: commands → validate → events (not direct updates!)
   CRUD pattern — in ES, you'd emit OrderStatusChanged event instead

hints: Events: past tense (Initiated, Filled, Failed, Created)
Commands: imperative (Process, Update, Place, Cancel)
State: derived in-memory — not stored in event sourcing

exercises.title: Complete the event sourcing aggregate
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks in the PaymentAggregate.

blanks.hint: apply each event to mutate aggregate state

hints: reconstruct: factory method that folds events into aggregate state
forEach(agg::apply): method reference — calls apply(event) for each event
State transition: each event type changes a specific field

exercises.title: PaymentAggregate with 3 event types
exercises.instructions:
Build PaymentAggregate:
1. Events: PaymentInitiatedEvent, PaymentProcessingEvent, PaymentFailedEvent
2. aggregate.status transitions: NEW → INITIATED → PROCESSING → FAILED
3. reconstruct(List<DomainEvent>) static factory
4. @SpringBootTest: replay 3 events → status is FAILED
hints: sealed interface: type-safe event hierarchy — compiler knows all subtypes
switch pattern matching (Java 17+): handle each event type cleanly
reconstruct returns NEW aggregate with events applied — no DB needed in test

exercises.title: Append-only event store
exercises.instructions:
Write an EventStore:
1. append(DomainEvent event): saves event (never updates)
2. loadEvents(String aggregateId): returns all events for that aggregate, ordered by sequence
3. In-memory implementation (List)
4. @SpringBootTest: append 3 events, load returns all 3 in order
hints: synchronized: thread-safe sequence increment (or use AtomicLong)
sorted by sequence: events must be in order for reconstruction
Append-only: NO update or delete methods in the store API

exercises.title: Temporal query — state at a specific point in time
exercises.instructions:
Implement temporal query on PaymentAggregate:
1. getStateAtEvent(List<DomainEvent>, int upToEventN) → PaymentAggregate
2. Replay only first N events
3. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): state at event 1=INITIATED, at 2=PROCESSING, at 3=FAILED
hints: stream().limit(N): replay only first N events
Temporal query: powerful for audit and debugging ('what was the state at time T?')
State at event 1: only PaymentInitiated applied → INITIATED

exercises.title: Snapshot for performance optimization
exercises.instructions:
Add snapshot support to PaymentAggregate:
1. AggregateSnapshot: stores aggregate state at event N
2. PaymentRepository: load(id) → check snapshot, replay events after snapshot
3. saveSnapshot(aggregate, eventCount): create snapshot
4. @SpringBootTest: snapshot at event 2, replay only event 3 → same final state
hints: Snapshot: store state at event N, replay only events after N
filter(sequence > snap.lastSequence): only events after snapshot
snapshotThreshold: take snapshot every 100 events (configurable)

exercises.title: Event store with PostgreSQL (append-only schema)
exercises.instructions:
Design PostgreSQL event store schema and Spring Data repository:
1. events table: id, aggregate_id, event_type, payload (JSONB), sequence_num, occurred_at
2. EventRecord JPA entity (no updates/deletes)
3. EventRecordRepository: findByAggregateIdOrderBySequenceNumAsc
4. Optimistic locking: unique constraint on (aggregate_id, sequence_num)
5. @DataJpaTest: append and load
hints: UniqueConstraint on (aggregate_id, sequence_num): prevents duplicate events (optimistic lock)
No update methods in entity: enforce append-only at the Java level
payload TEXT (or JSONB in PostgreSQL): store serialized event data

exercises.title: Event versioning and upcasting
hints: Events are immutable: upcast on-the-fly (don't modify stored events)
failureCode FRAUD → retryable=false: business rule in upcaster
Version registry: string-based version routing

exercises.title: Replay events to build a new projection
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay adds a new analytics requirement AFTER going live.
Replay ALL historical payment events to build a new FailureAnalyticsProjection:
1. FailureAnalyticsProjection: count failures per failureCode
2. replay(List<StoredEvent>) method: processes all historical events
3. @SpringBootTest: replay 10 events → correct failure counts
hints: Replay: process historical events to build NEW projections (added after go-live)
filter(PaymentFailed): only process relevant event types
Replay same code as live processing: projection is idempotent

exercises.title: Event sourcing vs CRUD trade-offs
exercises.instructions:
For each scenario, recommend Event Sourcing (ES) or CRUD and explain why:
A: User profile management (name, email, preferences)
B: Razorpay payment gateway (regulatory audit required)
C: Shopping cart (session-based, temporary)
D: Zerodha brokerage account positions (SEBI requires 7-year history)
E: Application configuration settings
exercises.expected_solution:
A: CRUD — User profile
   Profile is current state only: name, email, address.
   No business need for 'what was my name 2 years ago?'
   History: not required by law or business.
   ES overhead not justified for simple key-value data.

B: EVENT SOURCING — Payment gateway
   Regulatory: RBI requires complete payment audit trails.
   Dispute resolution: 'why did PAY-001 fail?' → replay events.
   Fraud detection: pattern in events (3 failures then success = fraud).
   Refund calculation: derive from original PaymentInitiated event.

C: CRUD — Shopping cart
   Ephemeral data: cart exists for hours/days (not years).
   No audit requirement: who cares what was in the cart 3 months ago?
   Performance: Redis-backed cart, fast reads/writes.
   ES complexity: massive overhead for throwaway data.

D: EVENT SOURCING — Brokerage accounts
   SEBI requires 7-year retention of all trade history.
   P&L calculation: derived from OrderFilled events (price × qty).
   Account reconstruction: replay all trades to get current positions.
   Corporate actions: dividend, split events change positions retroactively.

E: CRUD — Application config
   Config is just current values: feature flags, limits.
   Perhaps add a version/history table (not full ES).
   ES overhead excessive for low-frequency, simple key-value data.

test_cases.expected:
A: CRUD — User profile
   Profile is current state only: name, email, address.
   No business need for 'what was my name 2 years ago?'
   History: not required by law or business.
   ES overhead not justified for simple key-value data.

B: EVENT SOURCING — Payment gateway
   Regulatory: RBI requires complete payment audit trails.
   Dispute resolution: 'why did PAY-001 fail?' → replay events.
   Fraud detection: pattern in events (3 failures then success = fraud).
   Refund calculation: derive from original PaymentInitiated event.

C: CRUD — Shopping cart
   Ephemeral data: cart exists for hours/days (not years).
   No audit requirement: who cares what was in the cart 3 months ago?
   Performance: Redis-backed cart, fast reads/writes.
   ES complexity: massive overhead for throwaway data.

D: EVENT SOURCING — Brokerage accounts
   SEBI requires 7-year retention of all trade history.
   P&L calculation: derived from OrderFilled events (price × qty).
   Account reconstruction: replay all trades to get current positions.
   Corporate actions: dividend, split events change positions retroactively.

E: CRUD — Application config
   Config is just current values: feature flags, limits.
   Perhaps add a version/history table (not full ES).
   ES overhead excessive for low-frequency, simple key-value data.

hints: Regulatory audit requirements → strong ES signal
Ephemeral or simple current-state data → CRUD
Complex state derived from history → ES strength

exercises.title: Complete payment event sourcing with projection
exercises.instructions:
Build complete ES system:
1. PaymentAggregate: reconstruct from events, apply commands, track changes
2. InMemoryEventStore: append + load
3. PaymentStatusProjection: build status index from event replay
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): initiate → reconstruct correct state, projection index, temporal query
hints: getChanges(): new events from command execution — save these to event store
PAggregate.reconstruct: static factory — replays all events into fresh aggregate
limit(1): temporal query — only first event applied

exercises.title: Event sourcing + CQRS: payment system
exercises.instructions:
Combine Event Sourcing with CQRS:
1. ES: PaymentAggregate events stored in event store
2. CQRS: PaymentStatusProjection reads from event log
3. Controller: POST /payments (command, 202), GET /payments/{id}/status (query)
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): command stored, projection readable
hints: Event store IS the write database in ES
Projection reads from event store (no separate write DB needed for simple systems)
reduce: fold events into final status string

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay payment event sourcing
exercises.instructions:
Build complete Razorpay ES system:
1. 4 events: PaymentInitiated, ProcessingStarted, BankTimeout, PaymentFailed
2. PaymentAggregate: reconstruct + apply commands + track changes
3. JPA EventStore: append-only (EventRecord entity)
4. PaymentReadService: load aggregate, get current status
5. @DataJpaTest (3 tests): append, reconstruct, status is FAILED after all events
hints: countByAggregateId: next sequence number = count + 1
reconstruct from JPA entities: map eventType string → state transition
@DataJpaTest + @Import(service): test JPA layer with a service class

exercises.title: Event store with optimistic concurrency control
exercises.instructions:
Prevent concurrent modifications to same aggregate:
1. EventRecord: unique constraint on (aggregate_id, seq)
2. Save with expected version: throw ConcurrentModificationException if seq already exists
3. @DataJpaTest: concurrent save → second write fails correctly
hints: expectedVersion: caller says 'I expect N events to exist' — if not, conflict
UniqueConstraint on (agg_id, seq): DB-level protection against duplicates
countByAggId == expectedVersion: pre-check before save

exercises.title: Complete M24 T2 Event Sourcing synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M24 T2 synthesis:
1. PaymentAggregate: 3 events (Initiated, Processing, Failed) + reconstruct + changes
2. EventStore: append + load (in-memory)
3. Projection: status index from replay
4. Snapshot: store state + load with reduced replay
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): reconstruct, temporal query, projection index, snapshot works
6. All 4 pass
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M24 T2 Event Sourcing synthesis
hints: limit parameter: enables temporal queries without extra code
getChanges(): events generated by commands — save these
snapshot stores status+seq: load restores state, then replay events after seq
